name: The forest of Iraty and its heights – running in the Basque Country
name_original: La forêt d'Iraty et ses hauteurs — Courir au Pays basque
description:
The largest beech forest in Europe, the Iraty forest is one of the jewels of the Basque Country. Its 20,000 hectares are divided between France and Spain and bring together many remarkable plant and animal species: beeches, firs, deer, roe deer, etc.

Through this route, I suggest you immerse yourself in this thousand-year-old massif, before taking to the heights to admire its immensity. The loop of 22 kilometers and 1,200 meters of elevation gain starts from the Iraty chalets at the Bagargiak pass. This starting point is ideal since it includes parking, a restaurant, a shop and even the possibility of sleeping there.

From the first meters, the crests of Organbidexka suggest a magnificent panorama of the summit of the Basque Country: the Pic d'Orhy. A short climb takes you to the foot of Odeizugaña before plunging into the mysterious forest of Iraty. You evolve on a beautiful and long portion of descent which winds through the vegetation until you come out on a road.

A first bump devoid of trees is offered to you before crossing the road again. Once crossed, you undertake a long portion of ascent which leads you to a very beautiful ridge. You gradually discover the Mendibel, the Arthanolatze and the Pic des Escaliers, the highest point of the outing. These summits offer their share of vertiginous and infinite panoramas, while overlooking the majestic forest of Iraty.

From the summit a short descent leads you back into the forest. From then on, you only have a short climb left before returning to your starting point at the chalets.
tips: Very beautiful ridge a few steps from the Iraty chalets. It offers a superb view of the Pic d'Orhy on the horizon.

The Mendibel, which culminates at 1,411 meters above sea level, is located on a very beautiful ridge line on the heights of Iraty.

All of the different peaks on this ridge offer spectacular views while overlooking deep ravines.
The Arthanolatze culminates at 1,531 meters above sea level. It forms a unique belvedere that offers spectacular views of Soule and Basse-Navare in the Basque Country.

The Pic des Escaliers opens up a beautiful panorama of Soule and Basse-Navarre. A path on a broad ridge joins the Arthanolatze. It offers spectacular views: on one side the immense Iraty forest and on the other the Orhy peak, the first 2,000 meters of the Pyrenees.

The Iraty chalets are located at Bagargiak Pass at 1,327 meters above sea level. Attached to the village of Larrau, the chalets are located in the beautiful Iraty forest as well as on the GR 10 trail. There you will find something to eat and stock up on water.
